title: buildah mount command should list mounts when no arguments are given.
body:
buildah mount should work like the mount command and list all mount points
when no options are specified.

Need buildah umount to remove mount point from the database when a mount point
is umounted.

Signed-off-by: Dan Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: @nalind We have a b.Mounts structure which I am not taking care of right now, Should we just remove this since we don't allow the container image to be mounted in more then one postition now?

@rhatdan Yeah, the Mounts string slice in there is probably a leftover from when we dropped the various `--mount` flags, so I don't think we're doing anything with that any more.  Might as well lose it, then.

:sunny: Test successful - [status-redhatci](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/59dfec35c392238fded93d7e46ec8f026a2e9471.1496342651024814336/index.html)
Approved by: nalind
Pushing 59dfec35c392238fded93d7e46ec8f026a2e9471 to master...
